reviews.review_msg: If u want to experience the true the Definition of inconvenience, please take this course 100%. U will be blown away by how much she loves to ignore ur emails, not post grades till whenever she feels like posting, and give u lower mark on ur final grade than what u have. Unacceptable amount of inconvenience and lack of communication 1 after another
Good Prof, kind and respective. Her course needs to be a little easier in terms of course outline but overall she is solid
Best prof I've had so far at Sfu. Easy class if you keep up with lectures and readings.

Most disorganized prof I ever had at SFU. She made ARCH 131 a nightmare. The course was messy where some lectures would not play and were posted randomly and not consistent with the syllabus laid out. The quizzes would have questions that weren't even related to what we were learning for the week. She and the TAs barely responded to emails.
AMAZING prof. I love her so much! The kindest, sweetest lady doing super interesting research. Very helpful and willing to help you if youre struggling. No exams.

Kimberly Plomb is the best teacher, & even better for online, she is so great & flexible with online, she is one of the only teachers not to give me stress, & i really did learn a lot from her class that you'll use in life, & understanding infectious diseases. I cant stress it enough how great she was, wish sfu had more prof like her

Hands down the most amazing prof I have ever had at SFU. She is so caring and gives such interesting lectures. I took evolutionary medicine with her as an intersessional and enjoyed it a lot, she was extra accessible and understanding during the COVID-19 pandemic. Wish the class wasn't intersessional so I didn't have to say goodbye

Kim is by far the most amazing prof I've had. She is extremely helpful and trys her best to help you succeed in a challenging class.  If you show up to open lab, ask question, and read the book you'll do well!
